html:
Exclusive to:

Purple Groupers are a Fish Food Item introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC. They can be obtained by stocking Fish Farms with Roe, together with Pierrot Fish, Neon Quattro Fish, and Tropical Fish. They can be used in the Crock Pot, and count as 0.5 units of Meat and 1 unit of Fish. They are specifically required to cook the Tropical Bouillabaisse. Purple Groupers cannot be dried using a Drying Rack.

When consumed, Purple Groupers grant the player a speed increase of 2 and prevent the player from having their wetness increased or their boat damaged by both common and big waves for 30 seconds. The speed bonus will stack with the bonus from Pierrot Fish and other sources, but will not stack with the bonus from Tropical Bouillabaisse.

Cooked Purple Grouper

A Cooked Purple Grouper is a Purple Grouper that has been cooked over a Campfire, Fire Pit, Chiminea or Star. It can also be used in various Crock Pot recipes. Cooked Purple Grouper grants the same buffs as the uncooked variant, but restores slightly more health.

Usage

Tips

  • In addition to the wetness and damage protection, this food allows the player to receive the speed boost granted by riding waves regardless of which direction the boat hits the waves. Considering the 4x speed boost that the Encrusted Boat receives from hitting waves, this buff becomes very powerful at times when waves are ubiquitous such as the storms of hurricane season and encounters with bosses and whales.

Trivia

  • Purple Groupers were added to the Shipwrecked DLC more than a year after its official completion in the Home Sea Home update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
  • Purple Groupers are based on real-life groupers, a commonly eaten fish related to sea bass.

Gallery

  • Woodlegs standing next to a Purple Grouper and a Cooked Purple Grouper on the ground.
  • A Fish Farm stocking Purple Groupers, visible from the purple fish on the small sign.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

“Surf and turf, hold the turf.”
–Wilson, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“You look kinda dumb.”
–Willow, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“Wolfgang wish he swim like fish.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“A pitiful creature.”
–Wendy, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“I RESCUED IT FROM THE WATER. INGRATE”
–WX-78, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“Part of the Epinephelinae family.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“Looks like dinner.”
–Woodie, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“Truly an ugly creature.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“A beautiful fish. Even dead.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“Aegir shall nöt save yöu this day.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“Haha! What a silly face.”
–Webber, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“His doofiness is kinda endearing.”
–Walani, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“It couldn't be any fresher!”
–Warly, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“Ye got thet look t'ya.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“'TIS A FISHY FISHY”
–Wilba, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“Purple Glub Glub”
–Wormwood, when examining a Purple Grouper.
“It's a fish. Pretty common around here.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Purple Grouper.
Purple Groupers are a Fish Food Item introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC. They can be obtained by stocking Fish Farms with Roe, together with Pierrot Fish, Neon Quattro Fish, and Tropical Fish. They can be used in the Crock Pot, and count as 0.5 units of Meat and 1 unit of Fish. They are specifically required to cook the Tropical Bouillabaisse. Purple Groupers cannot be dried using a Drying Rack.
When consumed, Purple Groupers grant the player a speed increase of 2 and prevent the player from having their wetness increased or their boat damaged by both common and big waves for 30 seconds. The speed bonus will stack with the bonus from Pierrot Fish and other sources, but will not stack with the bonus from Tropical Bouillabaisse.

“That fish is fin-ished.”
–Wilson, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Fire burned the grossest bits right off.”
–Willow, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Wolfgang will eat tasty fish!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Rest now, fish spirit.”
–Wendy, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“NOW IT'S ROBOT FUEL. HA HA”
–WX-78, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Eating unprepared fish just wouldn't be safe.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Caught and cooked it myself.”
–Woodie, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Purple on the outside, pink in the middle.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Good, it still contains the bones. They're a great source of calcium!”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Thank Njörd för this gift!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“He likes the fish heads. I don't.”
–Webber, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Sorry, man. It was you or me.”
–Walani, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Pan-fried grouper with pigeon peas! Delectable!”
–Warly, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Ready fer m'gullet.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“FWOOSH'D FISHY YUM YUMS”
–Wilba, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“No more swimming”
–Wormwood, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
“Swell. The fins are still attached.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Cooked Purple Grouper.
A Cooked Purple Grouper is a Purple Grouper that has been cooked over a Campfire, Fire Pit, Chiminea or Star. It can also be used in various Crock Pot recipes. Cooked Purple Grouper grants the same buffs as the uncooked variant, but restores slightly more health.
Usage[edit | edit source]

In addition to the wetness and damage protection, this food allows the player to receive the speed boost granted by riding waves regardless of which direction the boat hits the waves. Considering the 4x speed boost that the Encrusted Boat receives from hitting waves, this buff becomes very powerful at times when waves are ubiquitous such as the storms of hurricane season and encounters with bosses and whales.

Purple Groupers are based on real-life groupers, a commonly eaten fish related to sea bass.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Cá Mú Tím là một Thực Phẩm Cá được giới thiệu trong DLC ​​Shipwrecked. Chúng có thể thu được bằng cách thả Trứng cá vào Trang trại Cá, cùng với Cá Pierrot, Cá Neon Quattro và Cá Nhiệt đới. Chúng có thể được sử dụng trong Nồi Hầm và được tính là 0,5 đơn vị Thịt và 1 đơn vị Cá. Họ được yêu cầu đặc biệt để nấu món Bouillabaisse nhiệt đới. Cá mú tím không thể sấy khô bằng Giá phơi.

'''Purple Groupers''' are a [[Fishes|Fish]] [[Food]] [[Item]] introduced in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC. They can be obtained by stocking [[Fish Farm]]s with [[Roe]], together with [[Pierrot Fish]], [[Neon Quattro]] Fish, and [[Tropical Fish]]. They can be used in the [[Crock Pot]], and count as 0.5 units of [[Meat]] and 1 unit of Fish. They are specifically required to cook the [[Tropical Bouillabaisse]]. Purple Groupers cannot be dried using a [[Drying Rack]].

When consumed, Purple Groupers grant the player a speed increase of 2 and prevent the player from having their wetness increased or their [[Boats|boat]] damaged by both common and big [[waves]] for 30 seconds. The speed bonus will stack with the bonus from [[Pierrot Fish]] and other sources, but will not stack with the bonus from [[Tropical Bouillabaisse]].

== {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* In addition to the wetness and damage protection, this food allows the player to receive the speed boost granted by riding waves regardless of which direction the boat hits the waves. Considering the 4x speed boost that the [[Encrusted Boat]] receives from hitting waves, this buff becomes very powerful at times when waves are ubiquitous such as the storms of hurricane season and encounters with bosses and whales.

=={{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* Purple Groupers were added to the ''Shipwrecked'' DLC more than a year after its official completion in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked#November 21, 2017 - Home Sea Home Update|Home Sea Home]]'' update, after being tested in a beta branch for several months.
* Purple Groupers are based on real-life groupers, a commonly eaten fish related to sea bass.
